---
title: Rural-nonrural wage gap remains wide
description: Average annual wages in 2023 real dollars for rural and nonrural counties,
  showing absolute dollar levels from 2007 to 2024.
date: '2026-05-07'
layout: default
permalink: /charts/rural-economic-outlook-x-dollar-wage-change-since-gr-lc/
categories: []
tags: []
image: X_dollar_wage_change_since_GR_lc.svg
chart:
  title: Rural-nonrural wage gap remains wide
  chartType: line
  interactive: false
  source: Bureau of Labor Statistics, U.S. Department of Labor
  sourceUrl: https://www.bls.gov/cew/
  featured: false
producedBy: rural-economic-outlook
usesDatasets:
  - qcew-employment-wages
usesPackages:
  - ruraldefinitions
usesResources: []
format:
  gfm:
    toc: false
    wrap: none
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

![Shows absolute real average annual wages (2023 dollars) for rural and nonrural counties from 2007 to 2024, placing the relative wage convergence in context: despite faster rural growth rates, the absolute dollar gap remains substantial.](./X_dollar_wage_change_since_GR_lc.svg)

## Overview

Shows absolute real average annual wages (2023 dollars) for rural and nonrural counties from 2007 to 2024, placing the relative wage convergence in context: despite faster rural growth rates, the absolute dollar gap remains substantial.

## Key Findings

- Nonrural average annual wages remain significantly higher in absolute terms than rural wages throughout the period.
- The absolute dollar gap has widened even as the relative gap narrowed, because both series grew but from different bases.
- Real wages in both geographies rose substantially from 2019-2022 amid COVID-era labor market tightening.
- All values are deflated to 2023 dollars using the BLS CPI-U-RS.

## Reproducibility

Generated by `R/viz/presentation/wage_change_lc.R` in the producing project.
